title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted object lifetime and address ledger, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in object lifetime and address ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Object Value Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 3266. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Const Volatile Limits
steps.instruction:
